title: 12 Reports and Reconciliation for CRA Business Taxes
body_text:
12	Reports and Reconciliation for CRA Business Taxes
12.1	Overview of Reports and Reconciliation
The information in this chapter is for financial institutions in BC and Ontario. Financial institutions outside of BC and Ontario follow reconciliation procedures with their provincial centrals.
Financial institutions are responsible for ensuring that the bill payments posted to their customers√Ę¬Ä¬ô accounts reconcile with the settlement totals processed by Central 1. Business tax transactions are included in the Bill Payments, Remitted (BPRE) and Bill Payments, Remitted Summary (BPRS) reports. See the Electronic Bill Payments System Part for more information on these reports.
You can download settlement reports from Central 1√Ę¬Ä¬ôs File and Report Exchange. If applicable, PDFs of these reports are available in the Banking folder. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information.
Postings can also be viewed on the Current Account Services (CAS) Online. See the Current Account Services (CAS) Online Part for more information.
Proceed as follows to balance bill payment transactions, daily:
Confirm that the transaction total on the BPRS report is the same as the settlement amount provided by Central 1. If the amounts don√Ę¬Ä¬ôt balance, check the Bill Payments Rejected Transactions (BPRT) report for the same date as the original transaction. Items that Central¬†1 rejected aren√Ę¬Ä¬ôt included in the Central 1 settlement amount.
Review the BPRE and BPRS reports. The posted settlement amount in CAS Online will match the totals in the BPRE and BPRS reports. These reports include CRA business transactions.
For more information on the BPRE, BPRT, and BPRS reports, see the Electronic Bill Payments Part.
12.2	Tax Payment Remitted Transactions (TPRE) Report
The Tax Payment Remitted Transactions (TPRE) report is available to assist financial institutions with reconciliation and settlement. The TPRE report has the same format as the BPRE report. The total of the TPRE report is included in the BPRS report tax totals.
Description
The TPRE report provides a list of in-branch CRA business tax transactions.
Purpose
The TPRE report displays CRA business tax transactions separately from other bill payment transactions.
Organization
One line is provided for each bill payment transaction. Transactions are sorted in customer account number order. Item counts and dollar amounts are provided in the lower section of the report.
Distribution
The report is produced daily and is available the business day after CRA business tax payments are received at Central 1.
BC financial institutions can access the TPRE report in the current Bill Payment report file (Bill file).
Ontario financial institutions can access the TPRE report in the following FTP folder: Bill_Pmt/TPRE_In_Branch_Tax_Remitted.
Sample
Figure 1: Sample TPRE Report
12.3	Tax Payments Monthly Billing (TXMC) Report
The Tax Payments Monthly Billing (TXMC) report is available to assist financial institutions with the reconciliation and settlement of their monthly charges. Monthly charges apply to any payment made to a CRA business tax vendor or to the Hydro/Revenue Quebec vendor.
Description
This report provides financial institutions with the monthly charges for all payments sent to a CRA business tax vendor or the Hydro/Revenue Quebec vendor. The report also provides the total number of transactions made and the total dollar amounts sent to each vendor.
Purpose
Use the report to validate the total monthly charges for all payments processed.
Organization
One line is provided for each vendor. Transactions are sorted by the vendor number. Item counts and dollar amounts are provided in the lower section of the report.
Distribution
The report is produced monthly.
Sample
Figure 2: Sample TXMC Report
END
